sections.paragraph:
κατώτερος, α, ον comp. of κάτω (s. two prec. entries and next entry; Hippocr. et al.; Vett. Val. 34, 21; IG XIV, 2476; Gen 35:8; TestLevi 3:1; ApcEsdr 4:5 p. 28, 4 Tdf. al.; Tat.) lower κατέβη εἰς τὰ κατώτερα μέρη τῆς γῆς he went down into the lower regions of the earth Eph 4:9 (on the expr. cp. Galen VIII 894 K. μέρη τῆς καρδίας κατωτέρω; Ps 138:15 ἐν τοῖς κατωτάτοις τῆς γῆς; Tob 13:2 S). Some think the pass. refers to Jesus’ burial. Many (e.g. Tert., Chrysostom, Bengel; Clemen2 90; JRobinson, Eph.) take τὰ κατ. μέρη τ. γῆς to be Hades (cp. Ael. Aristid. 26, 103 K.=14 p. 367 D. of the Titans: εἰς τ. κατωτάτους μυχοὺς τῆς γῆς ἀπελθεῖν; ApcEsdr 4:5). Others hold that Jesus’ coming on earth, the incarnation, is meant (s. lit. cited by MBarth, Eph [AB] ’74, 434 n. 49).—ALindroth, Descendit ad inferna: SvTK 8, ’32, 121–40. B-D-F §167. S. on πνεῦμα 2 and 4c.—M-M. TW.

comp. comp. = comparative(ly)

prec. prec. = preceding

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

expr. expr. = expression

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Tert Tert , II–III a.d. a.d.—List 5

Bengel Bengel =JB., Gnomon Novi Testamenti—List 6

Clemen Clemen = CC., Religionsgeschichtliche Erklärung des NT—List 6

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 535
